headline: Adobe announces $25 billion stock buyback amid AI disruption fears
summary: Adobe on Tuesday announced a share repurchase program worth up to $25 billion through April ‌30, 2030, as the Photoshop maker seeks to reassure ‌investors of its growth strategy amid the rise of creative autonomous tools.  Its ​shares rose around 2% in extended trading, but the stock has fallen around 30% this year as investors weigh the effects of new agentic models that many fear could hamper demand ‌for traditional software and ⁠design products such as those provided by Adobe.  "Our new $25 billion share repurchase authorization is a ⁠direct expression of confidence in our robust cash flow and the long-term value we are delivering to investors," said Adobe CFO ​Dan Durn.

raw.headline: Adobe announces $25 billion stock buyback amid AI disruption fears
raw.summary: Adobe on Tuesday announced a share repurchase program worth up to $25 billion through April ‌30, 2030, as the Photoshop maker seeks to reassure ‌investors of its growth strategy amid the rise of creative autonomous tools.  Its ​shares rose around 2% in extended trading, but the stock has fallen around 30% this year as investors weigh the effects of new agentic models that many fear could hamper demand ‌for traditional software and ⁠design products such as those provided by Adobe.  "Our new $25 billion share repurchase authorization is a ⁠direct expression of confidence in our robust cash flow and the long-term value we are delivering to investors," said Adobe CFO ​Dan Durn.

headline: Newsweek Names Integrity One of America's Greatest Workplaces for Culture, Belonging & Community
summary: Integrity, LLC ("Integrity"), a leading distributor of life and health insurance, and provider of wealth management and retirement planning solutions, today announced it has been recognized by Newsweek as one of America's Greatest Workplaces for Culture, Belonging & Community. This prestigious award honors the company's emphasis on creating an environment where employees feel valued, heard and connected. Other winners include American Express, Google, GE, Starbucks, 3M, Accenture and Adobe.

summary: SBI Growth Advisory today announced it has acquired The Brevet Group, a sales effectiveness and training firm. This strategic combination cements SBI's position as the industry's leading revenue growth and activation firm by integrating SBI's growth advisory services with Brevet's renowned delivery excellence and reputation for supporting successful commercial transformations.

headline: IBM Introduces Industry Solutions for AI‑Powered Experience Orchestration with Adobe
summary: Customer expectations are evolving faster than many organizations can respond. New global research from the IBM Institute for Business Value, conducted in partnership with Adobe, shows that companies lose an average of $29 million annually because they can't react quickly enough to customer demands. Three-quarters of surveyed executives say their companies are too slow to respond to changing customer needs.

headline: Adobe Summit Booth Tour With President David Wadhwani: Fortt Knox
summary: At Adobe Summit, Adobe President David Wadhwani and evangelist Paul Trani walk Jon Fortt through Firefly as an all-in-one AI creative studio. Paul demos Firefly Boards generating images of Jon in multiple styles; Precision Flow sliders for fine-tuning density and detail; and a new creative agent (launching in weeks) that auto-tones photos, colorizes black-and-whites, and outputs every social platform in one conversation. Wadhwani: "40 years of Adobe creativity and a wand."

headline: Adobe's Shantanu Narayen and Nvidia's Jensen Huang on CEO Transitions: Fortt Knox
summary: At Adobe Summit, weeks after announcing his CEO transition, Shantanu Narayen reflects with Jensen Huang. Huang calls Adobe unmatched at "how the world tells stories" and says its market opportunity just grew 100–1,000× with Firefly running on NVIDIA's stack — Omniverse, CUDA, Cosmos, NeMo — plus agentic AI encoding Adobe's marketing know-how into agents. Narayen's playbook: innovate, expand the lens, use M&A before the market sees the category.
